notes: Bronze DQ rules for EADA institution-totals (academic year 2022-2023, reporting_year=2022). Logical name `raw.eada` per the spec; physical Iceberg namespace is `bronze.eada` (matching the catalog convention used by sibling raw-ingest-* rule files). Source spec: docs/specs/full-pipeline-eada.md §4 (rules RAW-EAD-001..RAW-EAD-012, including the §8 amendment that added RAW-EAD-012). The EDA at governance/eda/full-pipeline-eada-raw-eda.md observed 2,040 rows with 100% non-null on all three monetary fields (no EADA suppression sentinels in the institution-totals file). Per the EDA recommendation, RAW-EAD-007 / RAW-EAD-008 / RAW-EAD-009 are tightened from spec-as-written (95% / 95% / 80%) to 99% to better reflect reality while leaving 1% headroom for one-off codebook drift in future cycles. RAW-EAD-012 is implemented as a 'row count == distinct UNITIDs (within 1%)' tripwire because the institution-totals file is a separate physical file and no in-pipeline filter is applied; this still catches any future per-team leak or over-filter regression. Per the §8 amendment, this file is named raw-eada.json (not raw-ingest-eada.json).

rules.name: Row count between 1,800 and 2,300
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bronze.eada) BETWEEN 1800 AND 2300 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: EADA institution-totals must contain ~1,800-2,300 reporting institutions per academic cycle. The window covers historical EADA cohort sizes since the 2014 reporting expansion. Below 1,800 means the ingestor dropped institutions (filter mis-fire, fetch truncation); above 2,300 means per-team rows leaked in.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-EAD-001 row count between 1,800 and 2,300 (P0, Volume)'. EDA 'Edge Cases' table: 'Total rows in 2022-23 institution file = 2,040 (100%). RAW-EAD-001 (1,800-2,300) holds with margin.' EDA Field Profiles: 'Record count: 2,040 institutions'. Threshold: count in [1800, 2300].

rules.name: unitid non-null (100%)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.eada WHERE unitid IS NULL
rules.description: unitid is the natural key of the EADA institution-totals file and the FK to every IPEDS-keyed downstream table. Null means a coercion failure in the ingestor's _coerce_long path or an upstream file corruption.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-EAD-002 unitid non-null 100% (P0, Completeness)'. EDA `unitid` field profile: 'Null Rate: 0% (0/2,040)'. EDA 'Edge Cases': 'unitid non-null and unique = 2,040/100%. RAW-EAD-002 + RAW-EAD-003 hold trivially.' Threshold: 0 nulls.

rules.name: unitid uniqueness (dedup grain)
rules.sql: SELECT unitid, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM bronze.eada GROUP BY unitid HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
rules.description: unitid is the declared dedup grain of bronze.eada (one row per institution per reporting year). Any duplicate indicates either a per-team leak (Schools.xlsx rows leaking into the institution-totals load) or a double-append to the Iceberg table.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-EAD-003 unitid uniqueness (P0, Uniqueness)'. Spec §4: 'Grain: UNITID. Dedup grain: [unitid]'. EDA `unitid` field profile: 'Cardinality: 2,040 distinct (100% unique -- confirms grain)'. Threshold: 0 duplicate unitid values.

rules.name: total_athletic_expenses >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.eada WHERE total_athletic_expenses IS NOT NULL AND total_athletic_expenses < 0
rules.description: Athletic expenses are a non-negative monetary amount by definition. Negative values are impossible in the EADA reporting framework; any negative value indicates an EADA suppression sentinel (-1 or -2) that escaped the ingestor's null-coercion path.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-EAD-004 total_athletic_expenses >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity)'. EDA `GRND_TOTAL_EXPENSE` field profile: 'min = $11,532 ... 0 rows below $0'. Spec §4 ingestor notes: 'Replace EADA suppression sentinels (blank, -1, -2) with NULL across all numeric fields before type coercion'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: total_athletic_revenue >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.eada WHERE total_athletic_revenue IS NOT NULL AND total_athletic_revenue < 0
rules.description: Athletic revenue is non-negative by definition. Negative values indicate an EADA suppression sentinel that escaped the ingestor's null-coercion path.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-EAD-005 total_athletic_revenue >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity)'. EDA `GRND_TOTAL_REVENUE` field profile: 'min = $11,532 ... 0 rows below $0'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: recruiting_expenses >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.eada WHERE recruiting_expenses IS NOT NULL AND recruiting_expenses < 0
rules.description: Recruiting expenses are a non-negative monetary amount. NOTE: 17.8% of institutions (363 / 2,040 in the 2022-23 cycle) report exactly $0 -- these are real reported zeros (NJCAA II/III, CCCAA, NWAC programs that don't recruit off-campus), NOT suppressions. This rule explicitly admits zero; do not tighten to '> 0'.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-EAD-006 recruiting_expenses >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity)'. EDA `RECRUITEXP_TOTAL` field profile: 'Zero rate: 17.8% (363 institutions report exactly $0). Edge case: the 363 zeros concentrate in NJCAA II/III, CCCAA, NWAC. They are real reported zeros (the institutions don't have a recruiting budget), not nulls.' EDA 'Edge Cases': 'recruiting_expenses == $0: 363, 17.8%. These are real zeros. Do NOT add a >0 rule. RAW-EAD-006 (>=0) is correct.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: total_athletic_expenses non-null >= 99%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN total_athletic_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM bronze.eada) >= 0.99 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Tightened from spec-as-written 95% to 99%. Observed completeness in the 2022-23 cycle is 100% (2,040 / 2,040 non-null) -- EADA's suppression sentinels are a per-sport phenomenon in Schools.xlsx and never appear in the institution-totals file (InstLevel.xlsx) we ingest. The 99% threshold honors the observed reality while leaving 1% (~20 row) headroom for one-off codebook drift in future cycles. The original 95% threshold was set far below observed reality and would not bind on any plausible regression.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-EAD-007 total_athletic_expenses non-null >= 95% (P0, Completeness)' -- tightened to 99% per EDA recommendation. EDA `GRND_TOTAL_EXPENSE` field profile: 'Null Rate: 0% (0/2,040). Zero suppression sentinels.' EDA 'Edge Cases' table: 'total_athletic_expenses non-null = 2,040/100%. RAW-EAD-007 (>=95% non-null) trivially holds; threshold is set far below observed reality, consider tightening to >=99%.' EDA 'Key Findings' #3: 'Zero suppression-sentinel hits in the institution-totals file. All three monetary fields are 100% non-null with no -1, -2, or blank values across 2,040 rows... This invalidates the threshold structure of RAW-EAD-007/008/009.' Threshold: non-null fraction >= 0.99.

rules.name: total_athletic_revenue non-null >= 99%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN total_athletic_revenue IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM bronze.eada) >= 0.99 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Tightened from spec-as-written 95% to 99%. Observed completeness is 100% (2,040 / 2,040 non-null). Same EDA evidence and rationale as RAW-EAD-007: institution-totals file carries no EADA suppression sentinels. 1% headroom retained for future-cycle codebook drift.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-EAD-008 total_athletic_revenue non-null >= 95% (P0, Completeness)' -- tightened to 99% per EDA recommendation. EDA `GRND_TOTAL_REVENUE` field profile: 'Null Rate: 0% (0/2,040)'. EDA 'Edge Cases' table: 'total_athletic_revenue non-null = 2,040/100%. RAW-EAD-008 (>=95% non-null) trivially holds; consider >=99%.' Threshold: non-null fraction >= 0.99.

rules.name: recruiting_expenses non-null >= 99%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN recruiting_expenses IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM bronze.eada) >= 0.99 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Tightened from spec-as-written 80% to 99%. EDA observed 100% (2,040 / 2,040) recruiting_expenses non-null in 2022-23. Note: 17.8% of those non-null values are exactly $0 (real reported zeros, not nulls -- see RAW-EAD-006). Completeness is about presence of a value, not whether the value is zero, so the 100% non-null observation stands. The original 80% threshold was dangerously loose -- it would tolerate ~400 newly-missing institutions before firing. P1 rather than P0 because recruiting_expenses is a non-required field per the §4 schema (`Required: no`).
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-EAD-009 recruiting_expenses non-null >= 80% (P1, Completeness)' -- tightened to 99% per EDA recommendation. EDA `RECRUITEXP_TOTAL` field profile: 'Null Rate: 0% (0/2,040)'. EDA 'Edge Cases' table: 'recruiting_expenses non-null = 2,040/100%. RAW-EAD-009 (>=80% non-null) trivially holds -- threshold is dangerously loose. Consider >=99%.' Field profile distinguishes the 363 real zeros from nulls explicitly. Priority kept at P1 because recruiting_expenses is non-required in the §4 schema. Threshold: non-null fraction >= 0.99.

rules.name: reporting_year is single value across all rows
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT reporting_year) FROM bronze.eada) = 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: EADA file does not carry an in-row reporting_year column; reporting_year is stamped by the ingestor from the academic-year-start derived from the cache filename (e.g., EADA_2022-2023.zip -> 2022). Every row in a single load must therefore carry the same reporting_year. Multiple distinct values means either two cycles were loaded into the same partition or the ingestor's pin logic broke. On annual refresh the value bumps in lockstep across the entire load.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-EAD-010 reporting_year is single value across all rows (P0, Consistency)'. EDA 'Anomalies' table: 'Reporting-year column = Absent. Year is encoded only in the filename. Pin reporting_year from the cache filename or constructor argument. RAW-EAD-010 (single-value rule) holds trivially because the ingestor stamps the constant.' EDA 'EadaIngestor Configuration Pin': 'DEFAULT_REPORTING_YEAR = 2022 (academic year start of 2022-23 cycle)'. EDA observed 2,040/2,040 = 2022. Threshold: exactly 1 distinct reporting_year.

rules.name: Spot check: at least one row total_athletic_expenses > $100M (D1 anchor)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bronze.eada WHERE total_athletic_expenses > 100000000) >= 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Sanity spot-check: every recent EADA cycle has multiple D1-FBS power-conference institutions reporting >$100M total athletic expense (Ohio State, USC, Notre Dame, Michigan, Texas, etc.). Zero such rows means either a per-team leak (where each row is a single-sport slice and the gross-anchor totals were dropped), a unit error (dollars vs thousands), or a fetch-truncation regression that lost the largest reporters. P1 because this is a plausibility tripwire: the magnitude check catches the gross-anchor failure mode that pointwise validity rules cannot.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-EAD-011 spot check: at least one row total_athletic_expenses > $100M (D1 anchor) (P1, Plausibility)'. EDA `GRND_TOTAL_EXPENSE` field profile: 'Histogram: $100M-200M = 56 rows; $200M-500M = 4 rows; max = $234,409,941 (Ohio State).' EDA 'Key Findings' #5: 'D1-FBS anchors confirmed for RAW-EAD-011. 60 institutions exceed $100M total expense; the top 5 are Ohio State ($234M), USC ($212M), Notre Dame ($204M), Michigan ($202M), Texas ($199M). RAW-EAD-011 passes with margin.' Threshold: at least 1 row > $100,000,000.

rules.name: Row count within 1% of distinct UNITIDs (filter regression tripwire)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bronze.eada) > 0 AND ABS((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bronze.eada) - (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT unitid) FROM bronze.eada)) <= GREATEST(1, CAST(0.01 * (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT unitid) FROM bronze.eada) AS BIGINT)) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Conservation tripwire: row count must equal distinct UNITID count to within 1% (or 1 row, whichever is greater). Per the §8 spec amendment and the EDA's resolution, the institution-totals file (InstLevel.xlsx) is a separate physical file and the ingestor applies no in-pipeline filter -- so today this rule degenerates to 'rows == distinct UNITIDs' (1:1, see RAW-EAD-003). It is retained as a P0 regression tripwire for any future code path that re-introduces a SPORTSCODE-style filter (where over-filter would drop institution rows AND per-team leak would produce N>UNITIDs). The 1% tolerance preserves the spec's original tolerance phrasing; the GREATEST(1, ...) clause prevents the threshold from collapsing to 0 on small loads.
rules.rationale: Spec §4 DQ Rules: 'RAW-EAD-012 post-filter row count is within 1% of distinct UNITIDs in raw (catches per-team leak AND over-filter) (P0, Conservation)'. Spec §4 EDA correction (2026-04-30): 'institution totals live in a separate file ... No in-pipeline filter is needed; the ingestor consumes InstLevel.xlsx directly. RAW-EAD-012 remains valuable as a regression tripwire (post-ingest row count must remain ~ distinct UNITIDs).' EDA 'Key Findings' #2: 'RAW-EAD-012 (post-filter row count within 1% of distinct UNITIDs) becomes a trivial pass under this model.' EDA 'EadaIngestor Configuration Pin': 'RAW-EAD-012 amendment recommendation: ... re-target it as ... a weaker conservation check.' EDA `unitid`: '2,040 distinct (100% unique)'. Today: 2,040 rows == 2,040 distinct UNITIDs, |diff|=0, passes. Threshold: |row_count - distinct_unitids| <= max(1, 0.01 * distinct_unitids).
